items: Prepared for Johnson, Cross and Gibbs
Client of record: {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}}
Engagement commenced {{fact:f:E-2020-001.start}}
Presented by Daniel Alvarado, Senior Consultant
For discussion with Angela Mahoney, General Manager, and finance staff
In scope: contribution margin by service line, volume and payer mix, revenue cycle drivers
Out of scope: audit of source data, rebuild of cost accounting, valuation of any compensation arrangement
Susan Clark, Principal, leads service line economics and owns the assumption log
Patricia Moore, Analyst, leads revenue cycle tracing
Daniel Alvarado documents compensation dependencies where a line's economics turn on one
Line list confirmed in writing with your office; we and you are working from the same list
Extracts received for most lines in scope; two remain outstanding and are logged
Contribution margin model built and populated for the first group of lines
Revenue cycle walkthroughs completed for two lines, in progress for the rest
Assumption log is open and available to you on request, not held to the end

blocks.text: How We Are Treating This Year

items: The disrupted months are not comparable to prior periods and will not be averaged in with them
Each line is presented twice: on the full trailing period, and on the unaffected periods alone
Where the two views disagree in direction, we say so rather than choosing the tidier one
The allocation basis behind both views is stated on the face of the model, not in an appendix
Where the data will not support a conclusion, that is what we will report
This year's trailing periods are not comparable; affected and unaffected months are shown separately throughout
At least one line's reported margin depends on a cost allocation we have not yet traced to source
Denials in one line appear to cluster at a single step; Patricia Moore is tracing it end to end before we characterize it
One line's cost structure depends on a physician arrangement outside our scope; the dependency is flagged, not evaluated
These are observations. They are not findings, and nothing here is a recommendation
Close the two outstanding extracts
Complete revenue cycle tracing across the remaining lines in scope
Bring the first findings, labeled as findings, to the next working session
Continue routing all requests through Angela Mahoney's office
Susan Clark will present the margin build and its assumptions when the model covers every line
